prestazioni: Engine swap to a Ford 302 Cleveland V8 (engine code: 351C) with a forged rotating assembly from Eagle Specialty Products (ESP), featuring a 4.030" bore, 3.750" stroke, and 11.5:1 compression ratio pistons (Mahle Motorsport). Paired with a Crane Cams hydraulic roller camshaft (232°/238° duration @ 0.050", 0.570"/0.570" lift) and Comp Cams roller rockers (1.6:1 ratio).

Forced induction via a Precision Turbo & Engine PT6266 CEA turbocharger (62mm inducer, 66mm exducer) with a custom T4 divided flange manifold by Burns Stainless, feeding into a Spearco air-to-water intercooler (1000cfm core) and a Holley Sniper EFI system (2x 1000cc injectors, dual-sync distributor). Boost controlled at 18psi via a TurboSmart Hyper-Gate45 wastegate.

Suspension overhaul with a full Global West subframe connectors kit, Hotchkis Sport 1" drop springs, and Bilstein B8 5100 series dampers (valved specifically for the Maverick’s 108" wheelbase). Rear axle upgraded to a Ford 9" with a Detroit Truetrac LSD, 31-spline axles (Moser Engineering), and a Currie Enterprises 9+ housing. Braking system includes Wilwood Dynalite 4-piston calipers (12.19" rotors front, 11.75" rear) with Hawk HT-10 brake pads.
estetica: Custom widebody conversion using a Rare Rides fiberglass kit (1970-73 Maverick-specific, 3" flares front/rear) with a custom-mixed BASF Glasurit 90-line paint in "Verde Urucum" (a Brazilian-inspired metallic green). Front bumper deleted and replaced with a custom aluminum tube bumper by Schwartz Performance, matching the rear Schwartz bumper with integrated dual exhaust exits (3.5" stainless steel, Borla Pro XS mufflers).

Interior stripped and reupholstered in Recaro SRD seats (cloth/Alcantara, Ford Performance blue stitching) with a custom roll cage by Autopower (8-point, mild steel, bolt-in). Dashboard replaced with a custom carbon fiber panel (by Fiberglass Unlimited) housing a Motec C127 dash display and a Nardi 380mm steering wheel (quick-release hub by NRG Innovations).

Aerodynamic package includes a custom front splitter (carbon fiber, 3" overhang) by APR Performance, paired with a custom ducktail spoiler (fiberglass, 5" height) by Vintage Air. Side skirts are OEM-style but modified with integrated LED turn signals (Luxx H8 bulbs) and a custom fuel filler door (aluminum, flush-mount) by Ringbrothers.
